career_angle: RFT skills are genuinely valuable -- but learn the technique from research papers first, then pick your vendor. Don't let AWS lock you into Bedrock when the same approach works on open-source stacks.

factors.rationale: First-party vendor blog. AWS writing about AWS products is marketing, not independent analysis.
Provides code snippets and a GSM8K walkthrough, but no independent benchmarks, no comparison to competitors, no failure modes discussed.
Step-by-step code is provided and GSM8K is public, but requires Bedrock access and Lambda setup -- vendor lock-in baked into reproducibility.
Maximum conflict. AWS selling AWS services. Every technical decision conveniently routes through Bedrock, Lambda, and IAM.
RLHF/RFT is well-established in ML research (Ouyang et al. 2022, DeepSeek-R1). The underlying science is sound and widely accepted.
Names specific models (GPT OSS 20B, Qwen 3 32B), specific dataset (GSM8K), and provides actual API calls. Concrete enough to verify.
AWS ML Blog delivers on technical claims but consistently oversells ease-of-use. Bedrock has had real adoption but also quiet feature deprecations.
